---
title: "Data Personas"
subtitle: "Data-driven Decision-making"
title-slide-attributes: 
  data-background-color: "#002147"
---

## Chantal {.center background-image="../../images/persona_data_01.png" background-size="cover" background-repeat="no-repeat" background-position="center"}

## {.center .smaller}

* AquaVolt Wakanda, the country’s oldest and largest private water and electricity utility, is still operating with legacy, manual processes that no longer match customer expectations.
* Newly appointed Director of Innovations Chantal has been asked to deliver visible modernization within 12 months, without disrupting service continuity.
* Key operational issues include manual meter reading, billing errors, delayed issue resolution, and outdated rules for planning new water and electrical line installations.
* The company needs a more data-driven model for field operations, resource allocation, and leadership reporting to improve efficiency and decision-making.
* Success will be measured by better billing accuracy, improved customer experience, faster field operations, and a credible foundation for future transformation.

## Jean Paul {.center background-image="../../images/persona_data_02.png" background-size="cover" background-repeat="no-repeat" background-position="center"}

## {.center .smaller}

* Jean Paul leads the **AeroHealth Evidence Lab** at Caledonia University, focusing on air quality research in the Department of Health and Environment.
* Over five years, his team has built a strong dataset using air quality sensors, traffic patterns, land use, and industrial location data.
* Jean Paul now wants to expand the research by linking environmental exposure data with health and health outcome datasets held by departmental colleagues.
* The main challenge is designing a secure, ethical, and methodologically sound research strategy for combining datasets across disciplines.
* Success would mean a scalable research program that produces evidence on how poor air quality affects health and supports policy and public health action.

## Marie {.center background-image="../../images/persona_data_03.png" background-size="cover" background-repeat="no-repeat" background-position="center"}

## {.center .smaller}

* Marie leads the **Poverty Alleviation Unit** after 30 years as a government social worker, and she wants to modernize how the unit serves vulnerable families.
* The unit has complete historical case records, but they are all paper-based, making it hard to search, analyze, and use the information strategically.
* Marie wants to turn those records into useful data to better understand client needs, identify trends, and improve welfare planning.
* A major challenge is that many staff are older, experienced, and cautious about moving from paper-based case work to digital systems.
* Success would mean a phased transition to digital case management that improves insight, efficiency, confidentiality, and service quality without disrupting frontline support.

## Alain {.center background-image="../../images/persona_data_04.png" background-size="cover" background-repeat="no-repeat" background-position="center"}

## {.center .smaller}

* Alain leads **Resilient Futures Alliance**, an NGO that supports families affected by natural disasters and captures detailed case data through a mobile field app.
* The organisation’s data is strong and consistent, recording household needs, assistance delivered, referrals, and follow-up across disaster responses.
* Alain wants to share this data with government departments so it can support broader analytics, coordination, and future disaster planning.
* The main challenge is building a secure, ethical, and practical data-sharing model that protects privacy, preserves trust, and works across multiple agencies.
* Success would mean a repeatable partnership framework that enables shared insights, better preparedness, and more coordinated disaster response and recovery.
